title: Delhi techie’s death at Mussoorie homestay: What happened between 3.30am and 7.30am?
content: In a tragic incident, 27-year-old P Radha Gayatri, a software engineer recently married, was found dead under suspicious conditions in a homestay in Dhanaulti. Her husband, Soumya Shricharan, discovered her lifeless body, which was naked and bleeding. The couple, who had reportedly indulged in alcohol before going to sleep, had just returned from a trip to Rishikesh.

title: 'Attacks stopped cold': FBI says it thwarted 'potential threat' to White House UFC event
content: The FBI announced the thwarting of an alleged plot targeting the UFC America 250 event at the White House. Multiple suspects were taken into custody following a multi-state operation after authorities became aware of the threat days before the event. Law enforcement agencies are credited with preventing planned attacks.

title: 'SL players should also be educated': India coach breaks silence on Sooryavanshi controversy
content: India A spin bowling coach Sairaj Bahutale addressed a post-match altercation involving 15-year-old Vaibhav Sooryavanshi after their Super Over loss to Sri Lanka A. Bahutale called the incident unfortunate, emphasizing the need for education on player conduct for both teams. He expressed confidence that Sooryavanshi, a young representative of India, would learn from the experience and avoid repeating it.

title: Michael Burry, whose fund is against Nvidia and Palantir, invests in Chinese stock
content: Michael Burry, famous for predicting the 2008 housing crash, has increased his stake in Alibaba, citing its AI strategy and buyback program as undervalued. He contrasts this with his bearish view on U.S. AI companies, warning of inflated valuations. Burry also expressed skepticism about Anthropic's nearly $1 trillion valuation, deeming its AI model business unsustainable at that price.

title: India builds record grain reserves as El Nino returns
content: India's grain reserves have reached unprecedented levels, with rice stocks soaring 15% year-on-year to a record high and wheat inventories hitting a five-year peak. This robust buffer strengthens national food security and offers flexibility for exports, even amidst El Niño concerns. Strong farmer procurement and favorable crop conditions have bolstered these impressive reserves.

title: Dharamsala gives wake-up call to rest of the BCCI state associations
content: Persistent rain in Dharamsala during the first ODI between India and Afghanistan highlighted the urgent need for advanced sub-surface drainage systems in Indian cricket stadiums. While HPCA Stadium and Chinnaswamy boast this technology, other venues rely on outdated methods, leading to significant delays and fan disappointment.

title: Dell CEO Michael Dell makes one of largest public university donations in US history
content: Michael Dell has gifted a staggering $750 million to the University of Texas at Austin, propelling the creation of a groundbreaking AI-native hospital and research campus. This substantial donation, adding to his family's legacy of support, will also bolster scholarships, advanced computing, and vital medical research, underscoring Dell's enduring connection to his alma mater.

title: Mamata files plea in Calcutta high court challenging Bhabanipur poll result
content: Mamata Banerjee has challenged her Bhabanipur assembly election defeat in the Calcutta High Court. She lost the seat, previously her stronghold, by 15,105 votes to BJP leader Suvendu Adhikari. This challenge follows her earlier narrow loss in Nandigram during the 2021 Assembly elections.

title: Sooryavanshi shoving incident: BCCI's Devajit Saikia asks players to...
content: BCCI secretary Devajit Saikia urged India A players to stay focused on the tri-series after Vaibhav Sooryavanshi's on-field altercation with Sri Lanka A's Vishen Halambage following a Super Over defeat in Dambulla. Sources claimed sledging triggered the clash, while the India A camp was also unhappy with several umpiring decisions, alleging biased officiating during the match.

title: Will Lionel Messi play today? Argentina Star’s injury update ahead of Algeria match
content: Lionel Messi is expected to start for Argentina against Algeria in the FIFA World Cup 2026 opener after recovering from a minor hamstring issue. Lionel Scaloni has provided a positive fitness update as Argentina prepares to begin its title defense in Kansas City. Messi’s availability also puts him on course to make history with a record sixth FIFA World Cup appearance.

title: Qualcomm CEO Cristiano Amon has a message for all smartphone users
content: Qualcomm CEO Cristiano Amon revealed the company is developing over 40 AI-powered device designs, including smart glasses and wearables, aiming to introduce intelligent 'agents' that will manage tasks across apps. These agents are poised to become the new center of digital life, shifting focus away from smartphones and potentially elevating smart glasses to smartphone-like scale.

title: 10 years of Smart Cities Mission: How technology is transforming urban India
content: India's urban areas are rapidly modernizing, driven by the Smart Cities Mission. Over 90% of planned projects are complete, transforming cities with technology for better mobility, safety, and services. Integrated Command and Control Centres are now operational in all 100 cities, enhancing data-driven governance and citizen well-being.

title: Zepto IPO: Can 10-minute delivery survive public-market scrutiny?
content: Zepto is preparing for its Initial Public Offering, revealing its rapid expansion and operational strengths in India's quick-commerce market. The company has achieved significant order volumes and user growth. Investors are closely examining its potential for sustainable profits amidst fierce competition and high operational costs. The IPO will test whether Zepto can translate its scale into long-term financial success.

title: Who is Sarpreet? First Indian origin player to start a FIFA World Cup match
content: New Zealand's Sarpreet Singh became the first player of Indian origin to start a FIFA World Cup match, playing nearly the full game in a 2-2 draw with Iran. Born to Punjabi parents, the 27-year-old previously made history with Bayern Munich. Other Indian-origin players at the tournament include Australia's Nishan Velupillay, DR Congo's Samuel Moutoussamy and Qatar's Tahsin Mohammed Jamshid.

title: 6.3-magnitude earthquake strikes northwest China, strong aftershocks follow
content: A powerful 6.3-magnitude earthquake jolted Qinghai Province's Haixi Prefecture on Tuesday evening. While no immediate damage or casualties were reported, authorities are actively assessing the impact in the high-altitude, sparsely populated area. Multiple aftershocks, including one around magnitude 5, have been recorded, and emergency teams are on alert as seismic activity continues to be monitored.

title: Kuwait launches 15-year residency scheme for foreign investors
content: Kuwait has introduced a new 15-year residency program for eligible foreign investors and senior executives, a significant policy shift aimed at attracting global capital. This initiative seeks to provide greater certainty for long-term business planning and encourage foreign direct investment, supporting economic diversification beyond oil revenues.

title: Vijay Raghavendra and Sriimurali's mother Jayamma Chinnegowda passes away
content: The Kannada film industry is in mourning following the passing of Jayamma Chinnegowda, mother of actors Vijay Raghavendra and Sriimurali. She died on June 16 due to age-related health issues. A film producer herself, Jayamma was a respected figure in Sandalwood and part of a prominent film family with ties to the legendary Dr. Rajkumar.

title: Virat Kohli spotted giving Akaay a piggyback ride during secret family outing
content: Away from the cricket pitch, Virat Kohli is cherishing family time during his injury recovery. Viral photos show the star batter enjoying a relaxed outdoor outing with his son Akaay and wife Anushka Sharma. Kohli is rehabilitating a hamstring tear sustained during the IPL final and is expected back for the England series.

title: B-52s, Tu-95s & H-6s: Why lumbering bombers still matter in supersonic, stealth era
content: Despite advanced stealth aircraft, large, slow bombers like the B-52, Tu-95, and H-6 remain vital. Their immense range and payload capacity allow for powerful conventional and nuclear strikes, even with modern upgrades. While vulnerable to sophisticated air defenses, their cost-effectiveness and ability to project global power ensure their continued relevance as strategic deterrents.

title: Tennis queens in 40s, Serena and Venus Williams reunite for Wimbledon doubles
content: Serena and Venus Williams will reunite for doubles at Wimbledon after receiving a wild card entry, marking their first partnership since the 2022 US Open. The sisters have won 14 Grand Slam doubles titles, including six at Wimbledon. Serena's comeback has also sparked speculation about a singles return, while Stan Wawrinka, Grigor Dimitrov and Nick Kyrgios received wild cards.

title: China’s Four Front Dilemma
content: China faces a critical 'Four Front Dilemma' with vulnerabilities on its Pacific and Himalayan borders. A resurgent Japan, a defiant Taiwan, a volatile Korean Peninsula, and a strengthened India create significant strategic challenges. Internal PLA weaknesses and a lack of combat experience further complicate Beijing's position, limiting its aggressive options.

title: Twisha Sharma death case: Husband, mother-in-law get 14-day custody extension
content: A Bhopal court has extended the judicial custody of Samarth Singh, husband of the deceased model Twisha Sharma, and his mother, former judge Giribala Singh, by 14 days. The extension is in connection with Twisha's death and an alleged dowry harassment case. The CBI is investigating the matter, following allegations of irregularities in the initial probe.

title: Watch: This sweeper operates at 80km/h on China roads; video goes viral
content: A high-speed road-cleaning machine in China has gone viral for its impressive efficiency and futuristic design, clearing debris at up to 80 km/h with minimal traffic disruption. This innovation highlights China's expanding use of robotics beyond industrial settings, with a new household robot also demonstrated performing domestic chores.

title: MPBSE high school second exam result 2026 out: Here's how to check scores
content: The Madhya Pradesh Board of Secondary Education (MPBSE) announced the High School Second Examination 2026 results today at 4 PM. Students can check their scores online via the board's portal using their credentials. This examination offers a second chance for students under the board's revised framework.

title: 'Operation Tiger': Amid defection buzz, Uddhav summons Sena (UBT) legislators on June 22
content: Amid defection rumors, Uddhav Thackeray has convened a crucial meeting of Shiv Sena (UBT) legislators on June 22. This comes as Shinde faction leaders claim their 'Operation Tiger' to bring over Sena (UBT) MPs and MLAs is in its final stages. Sanjay Raut has dismissed these claims, accusing the BJP of orchestrating splits.

title: Williamson: How the first Fab 4 member to bow out stacked up against Kohli, Root & Smith
content: For years, the Fab Four debate searched endlessly for a winner. As Kane Williamson becomes the first member of the quartet to retire, this is not a ranking but a reflection. Amid Kohli's intensity, Smith's absurdity and Root's volume, Williamson quietly went about his business. From match-winning runs and captaincy highs to carrying the heaviest individual load, the New Zealander built a career defined by greatness without the noise.

title: Pakistani filmmaker showers praise on Imtiaz Ali's 'Main Vaapas Aunga'
content: Imtiaz Ali's 'Main Vaapas Aaunga' resonates deeply by exploring Partition's emotional scars through a personal romance. Pakistani filmmaker Umar Nasir Ali praised the film, noting its themes of memory and belonging echo his own upcoming project. The story follows a Sikh family's migration and a young man's enduring love, culminating in a poignant return to ancestral lands decades later.

title: 'Had to take drastic step as ...': NTA chief on temporary Telegram ban ahead of NEET retest
content: In a decisive effort to safeguard students from potential scams, the National Testing Agency has put a temporary hold on Telegram. This action comes as a response to rampant misuse of the platform by fraudsters distributing counterfeit NEET exam papers.

title: ‘Drugged, raped, filmed’: Nagpur woman alleges abuse, 'hypnosis’ during forced conversion
content: A video surfaces showing a woman pleading to be released while a man chants religious verses, allegedly linked to her claims of rape, blackmail, and forced religious conversion in Nagpur. The woman alleges her former acquaintance, Ayyaz Taj Madare, subjected her to rituals, spiked her drink, and extorted money before forcing a conversion and marriage.

title: Anurag Kashyap slams theatres for 'killing' films like 'Bandar' and 'Main Vaapas Aaunga'
content: In a new interview, filmmaker Anurag Kashyap has critiqued the lack of screens for Indian films like Imtiaz Ali’s 'Main Vaapas Aaunga' and his own film 'Bandar,' compared to Hollywood releases like 'Obsession'. Kashyap revealed he gets upset when fans praise his films years later on OTT, noting they never supported them in theatres. The filmmaker warns that prioritizing massive event movies like 'Dhurandhar' over word-of-mouth films is a dangerous financial strategy for cinema halls.

title: Exercise Pitch Black: India to participate in one of the largest aerial wargames in Australia
content: The Indian Air Force is actively participating in Exercise Pitch Black 2026 in Australia's Northern Territory, a major multinational aerial wargame. Alongside over 100 aircraft and thousands of personnel from 20 nations, the IAF will engage in complex combat scenarios, emphasizing night-time flying. This exercise, a cornerstone of international air cooperation, allows for vital skill exchange and operational integration.

title: Gujarat’s Halpati Housing Scheme offers Rs 1.2 lakh aid: Eligibility & how to apply
content: Gujarat's Halpati Housing Scheme offers crucial financial aid of Rs 1.20 lakh to eligible Scheduled Tribe families, specifically Talaviya and Nayak communities, for constructing their own homes. This initiative, implemented by the Tribal Development Department, aims to improve housing conditions in selected South Gujarat districts by providing phased assistance directly to beneficiaries, easing the burden of rising construction costs.

title: Watch: Ukraine hits Moscow region's largest refinery in deep-strike attack
content: Ukraine has struck an oil refinery in Moscow's region with a drone, causing damage. President Zelenskyy highlighted Ukraine's extended strike capabilities, emphasizing this as a response to Russian attacks and a means to pressure Moscow to end the war. This incident marks a significant escalation in Ukrainian strikes targeting Russian energy infrastructure, impacting fuel production.

title: Low-flying aircraft damages yet another Kochi house, airport plans compensation
content: Cochin International Airport is considering using CSR funds to compensate homeowners whose houses are damaged by low-flying aircraft. This follows repeated incidents, with the latest involving roof tiles falling from a house in Nedumbassery. The DGCA will investigate, and Cial aims to provide humanitarian compensation due to the complex process of claiming from airlines.

title: Second wife denied family pension even after first wife’s death; what court said
content: A Punjab woman, the second wife of a deceased government employee, has won a legal battle for her full family pension. The High Court ruled she is entitled to 100% of the pension, rejecting the state's claim to cap it at 50%. The court found the state's interpretation of rules was incorrect, as she was the sole surviving widow.

title: A bird strike away from disaster? Pune air station’s garbage menace raises aviation safety fears
content: Pune's Air Force Station and civilian airport face a grave bird strike threat due to persistent civic negligence. Open garbage dumping and unscientific waste management create ideal bird habitats, increasing collision risks. Despite repeated warnings and documented incidents, the Pune Municipal Corporation's failure to enforce cleanliness poses a significant aviation safety and national security hazard.

title: TCS to take $70 million hit as US Supreme Court rejects appeal - What's the case?
content: TCS said it will now recognise a further $70 million towards damages, interest obligations and legal expenses, which will be reflected as a one-time exceptional charge in its first-quarter FY27 financial results.

title: ‘Involuntary twitch’: Why FIFA cleared WC referee over alleged 'neo-Nazi' gesture
content: FIFA has cleared Australian video review official Shaun Evans of any wrongdoing after investigating a hand gesture resembling a white supremacist symbol. The governing body's disciplinary committee found no evidence of a breach, accepting Evans' explanation of an unintentional, subconscious twitch. Anti-discrimination groups had called for his removal, but FIFA's ruling allows him to continue at the World Cup.

title: Flight of the 'seagull': Valentina Tereshkova became the first woman in space 63 years ago today
content: On June 16, 1963, Valentina Tereshkova, a 26-year-old textile worker, became the first woman in space aboard Vostok 6. Her flight, a political statement by Nikita Khrushchev, lasted nearly three days, completing 48 orbits. Tereshkova's achievement, despite lacking formal pilot or engineering training, was celebrated globally, though she never flew again.

title: Forest Minister Shibu Baby John assures support for child injured in Chinnakkanal elephant attack
content: Ration supplies will be delivered to the family’s home until Rakshan attains the age of 18. The children, who have lost both their parents, will also receive a monthly assistance of ₹5,000.

title: Dattagalli blaze: Hotel owners’ association seeks ₹50 lakh relief for kin of deceased workers
content: The association also seeks a comprehensive review of safety standards and regulatory oversight in pubs and restobars across the city

title: Central agencies join Operation Toofan to intensify anti-drug crackdown in Kerala
content: According to the Home Minister, Operation Toofan has already registered immense success within two weeks of its launch.

title: Tamil Nadu CM Vijay inaugurates two-day training programme for MLAs
content: Former Assembly Secretaries and officials will train the MLAs on the Rules of Procedure of the Assembly and on the functioning of specific government departments. Speaker J.C.D. Prabhakar said 146 MLAs were first-time legislators and several of them were youngsters.

title: Kanipakam temple bookings now on Mana Mitra WhatsApp platform
content: Devotees can book darshan, arjitha sevas and prasadam tickets, and make donations, through the Mana Mitra WhatsApp number 9552300009 or by scanning QR codes at the temple

title: UDF reaches fragile truce in Pala amid power-sharing row
content: With coalition partners revising their power-sharing arrangement ahead of a crucial municipal council meeting on Wednesday

title: AIADMK leader C. Vijayabaskar resigns his MLA post
content: He is the fifth AIADMK legislator to resign from the Assembly weeks after the Assembly elections in 2026.

title: What are the Supreme Court guidelines for protecting survivors of human trafficking? | Explained
content: What was the judgment about? What is the existing legal framework? What does it say about voluntary sex work? What is the survivors’ right to rehabilitation?

title: Rabri Devi seeks more time to vacate official residence, cites Lalu’s health
content: RJD leader writes to Bihar government after deadline to vacate 10, Circular Road expires; says special arrangements are needed for Lalu Prasad

title: JSP forms committee to oversee induction of leaders, intellectuals into the party
content: The 14-member Joinings Committee will decide who should be inducted into the party and ensure that it does not lead to dissent or dissatisfaction among the existing cadres

title: KSEB services must be people-friendly: Sunny Joseph
content: Minister issues several directives to improve operational efficiency and strengthen monsoon preparedness

title: PM Modi arrives in France to attend G7 Summit
content: Mr. Modi said that India “remains committed to advancing collective efforts for a more sustainable and prosperous planet”

title: NIA attaches Amritsar property in LeT-linked narco-terror case
content: The proceeds from the trafficking of narcotic substances were used to finance and support terrorist activities in India through a complex and elaborate financial network, the National Investigation Agency says

title: BJP government has ‘failed’ first test of accountability by not seeking Pradhan's resignation, says Kharge
content: The Modi government has temporarily blocked Telegram for the NEET exam re-test; Modi ji should first demand the resignation of his Education Minister Dharmendra Pradhan, says Congress president Mallikarjun Kharge

title: NCPI ready to accept merger of rebel TMC MPs; numbers may go up to 22: Kakoli Dastidar
content: “Acceptance has already come to us. They [NCPI] are happy to take us. We will work together with the NDA under the leadership of Prime Minister Narendra Modi and Home Minister Amit Shah,” says Kakoli Dastidar

title: Woman held for attempted gold chain theft amid Kottiyoor fest
content: The accused, Jyothi, was allegedly caught red-handed while trying to snatch a gold chain from a devotee

title: Tamil Nadu CM Vijay orders establishment of 231 new electricity substations at ₹15,032 crore
content: The Chief Minister also directed that 15,058 workers be appointed for repair and maintenance activities.

title: BJP leader alleges Abhishek Banerjee’s link to soil pilferage racket; files police complaint
content: The BJP leader had approached Bishnupur Police Station in the southern suburbs of Kolkata, accusing Abhishek Banerjee of misappropriating ₹250 crore from a central government relief fund meant for Cyclone Amphan victims

title: India’s U.S. dilemma as ties turn taxing, benefits shrink
content: Countries that partnered with the United States over the decades subordinated their strategic autonomy, and even national sovereignty, for the benefits that came with it

title: Beyond high temperatures: understanding heat stress in Tamil Nadu
content: It is no longer only about how high the temperature soars. As Tamil Nadu rolls out a Heat Action Plan and meteorologists track El Nino, attention is shifting towards understanding heat stress and how communities can adapt to a warming future

title: Tamil Nadu unveils action plan to reduce heat-induced illnesses
content: The plan recommends solutions for heatwave management, and integration of recommended solutions into State programmes, among other measures.

title: AIADMK rival factions reconcile two weeks after Legislature Party split
content: The factions led by Edappadi Palaniswami and S.P. Velumani, respectively, withdraw disqualification petitions submitted to Speaker

title: Decision will reflect wishes of Viralimalai constituency voters, says Vijayabaskar
content: Vijayabaskar was one of the AIADMK rebel MLAs who voted in favour of the confidence motion moved by Chief Minister C. Joseph Vijay in the Assembly recently

title: Woes of Sri Lankan refugees seem to be never ending; they look for tangible help from the new govt in T.N. and Centre
content: With no bank account or any identity papers, the refugees who take up small jobs are often at the mercy of local agents who charge a commission for carrying out even basic financial transactions; there is a question mark over the future of children of these migrants

title: Twisha Sharma dowry-harassment case: A timeline of events
content: The high-profile dowry-harassment case involving 33-year old Twisha Sharma in Bhopal has developed into a story of alleged institutional bias, procedural lapses and evidence tampering.

title: Illegally parked vehicles hold city’s neighbourhood roads  hostage
content: Residents say roadside parking by private vehicles is disrupting traffic, endangering pedestrians and schoolchildren, and undermining the quality of life in urban neighbourhoods

title: Twisha Sharma death case: Court extends judicial custody of husband, mother-in-law
content: The CBI had sought an extension of judicial custody until June 30, which the court accepted. Both accused are currently lodged in the Bhopal Central Jail

title: Star Air launches direct flights to Mundra, operations to commence from June 23
content: The airline said it will operate five weekly direct flights between Mumbai and Mundra ensuring frequent and convenient access for travellers

title: Mamata files plea in Calcutta HC challenging poll result in Bhabanipur Assembly seat she lost
content: Suvendu Adhikari, now the first BJP CM of Bengal, defeated the TMC chief by a margin of 15,105 votes in the seat

title: North Bengal development roadmap ready, all Central schemes will reach hills: CM Suvendu
content: A comprehensive roadmap for the development of North Bengal has already been prepared; all welfare and development schemes of the Centre will reach the people of the hills, says Chief Minister Suvendu Adhikari

title: BJP weighs organisational restructuring amid rapid expansion
content: Party discusses regional in-charges, expansion of office-bearers and organisational changes ahead of new national team and possible Cabinet reshuffle

title: Bangladesh PM’s adviser returns from Delhi airport; says he was ‘humiliated’ by immigration
content: Rahman was held for more than two hours before he was cleared to enter Delhi; by that time, he had made up his mind to return

title: Conservation activists voice concern over Theppakadu Elephant Camp modernisation plan
content: The estimated ₹35 crore project is being designed to upgrade the more than century-old camp that houses 27 elephants. Conservation biologist and member of the National Board for Wildlife, Priya Davidar, said she has “strong misgivings” as it involves construction in the core area of a tiger reserve and crucial elephant habitat.

title: Google data centre will not cause water or power crisis in Vizag, says MP Sribharat
content: Sribharat said the vizag’s supply could rise to 2,110 MLD with Polavaram and that the project would rely mainly on renewable energy

title: AMMA row: Police record statements of Tini Tom, Shweta Menon on actor Ansiba Hassan’s complaint
content: Police sources say Tini Tom refuted making any communal remarks against Ansiba and accused her of filing the complaint out of ‘personal vendetta’

title: Subhash Palekar seeks ‘U-turn’ from Green Revolution-era farm policies in Andhra Pradesh
content: The Padma Shri awardee and natural-farming exponent said chemical residue in soil could be neutralised in a year through natural methods

title: Supreme Court seeks responses of Centre, States on plea for use of Aadhaar as identity proof only
content: The petition contended that the existing verification mechanism under Form-6 is inadequate and might allow individuals without proper supporting documents to be included in the electoral database

title: Four transgender persons arrested for robbing motorist near Sira on NH-48
content: The alleged incident took place in the early hours of June 14 when the complainant had stopped his car at a roadside rest area on the Tumakuru-Hiriyur stretch of NH-48 to take a break

title: Settlement pact signed in Pariyathukavu land dispute
content: The agreement was signed by the seven Dalit families residing at the Pariyathukavu at Malayidamthuruth near Kizhakkambalam and the heirs of the late Kannatt Sankaran Nair, whose claim over the land where the former have been settled was established through a prolonged legal battle

title: NEET-UG re-exam: Telegram app restricted in India at NTA request
content: The agency said that several Telegram groups were advertising fake exam papers for the upcoming NEET exam and defrauding candidates willing to pay for them

title: Defected MPs 'gaddar team', TMC is led by Mamata: Saugata Roy
content: He said the Trinamool Congress secured nearly 41% of the votes in West Bengal in the recent Assembly elections despite what he described as attempts by the BJP to misuse central agencies

title: Annamalai criticises NEET re-test arrangements
content: In a social media post, Mr. Annamalai criticised the arrangements made by the Ministry of Education for the NEET re-test scheduled for June 21.

title: New Zealand's Sarpreet becomes first player of Indian origin to start a FIFA World Cup match
content: Born in Auckland to Punjabi parents, the 27-year-old Sarpreet made his World Cup debut in the politically-charged match and had a few attempts at the Iranian goal

title: ‘Varanasi in Bratislava’: PM Modi visits unique exhibition crafted by Slovak artisans at Presidential Palace
content: The project aimed to foster cultural dialogue through visual art and offer Slovak artists an opportunity to explore and interpret "Varanasi," one of the world's oldest living cities and a timeless symbol of India's spiritual heritage

title: Amit Shah masterminded Trinamool ‘split’, merger bid, says Congress
content: Jairam Ramesh says the move is aimed at engineering two-thirds majority for the NDA in the Lok Sabha.

title: Kakoli seeks Kalyan’s expulsion from Lok Sabha, writes to Speaker
content: Rebel Trinamool Congress MP Kakoli Ghosh Dastidar wrote to Speaker Om Birla and cited repeated verbal abuse and misogynistic conduct during House proceedings allegedly by Kalyan Banerjee

title: False propaganda, person in video not me: Mann on Akal Takht edict against him
content: In his video message on Tuesday (June 16, 2026), Punjab CM Bhagwant Mann said an edict had been issued against him over the video, claiming that it featured him and that it was not AI-generated

title: Akal Takht vs Bhagwant Mann: What It Means For AAP Ahead Of 2027 Punjab Polls
content: With Punjab heading to the polls in 2027, this declaration by the highest temporal authority of Sikhs carries massive moral weight and threatens to fundamentally alter the electoral landscape.

title: FBI Foiled Plot To Attack White House During UFC Event: Kash Patel
content: The "UFC Freedom 250" event -- which also fell on Trump's 80th birthday -- was meant to kick off this year's festivities for the 250th anniversary of US independence.

title: Punjab Inmate Lands In More Trouble Over Reel Alleging Harassment In Jail
content: The case was registered on the complaint of jail Assistant Superintendent Jagwinder Kumar, police said.

title: US Military To Build War-Ready Weapons Stockpile In Australia
content: The development of the stockpile, a first for the Marine Corps in Australia, comes as the United States is keen to leverage the continent's strategic location in the South Pacific to counter China's rapid military build-up, analysts said.

title: Actor Mohanlal To Join Kerala's Anti-Drug Crackdown "Operation Toofan"
content: Operation Toofan will be implemented in three phases, "Toofan Strike", "Toofan Warriors" and "Toofan Care". Central agencies like the railway police and CISF among others will also join the initiative.

title: 'If Israel Cannot Handle Hezbollah, Syria Will Do The Job': Trump
content: "If Israel can't do the job (against Hezbollah) without killing everyone else, than he (Sharaa) will do the job. Syria will do the job," Trump said.

title: Video: Lorry, Tractor, Bolero - A Three-Way Crash On Karnataka Highway
content: After the initial impact, the lorry reportedly veered onto the opposite carriageway and crashed into a Bolero coming from the other direction.

title: Delhi Techie, On Honeymoon, Found Dead By Husband In Pool Of Urine And Blood
content: P Radha Gayatri was on a trip with her husband, Soumya Sricharan, who also works in the IT sector but in Pune.

title: G7 Summit 2026 LIVE Updates: PM Modi Arrives In France, Will Meet Trump Tomorrow
content: G7 Summit 2026 LIVE Updates: This is India's 13th participation in the G7 as a guest nation and PM Modi's seventh consecutive appearance at the summit.

title: "Won't Withdraw, Won't Commit Suicide": Israel Envoy On Action In Lebanon
content: Israel envoy said Tel Aviv would not withdraw from its buffer zone in southern Lebanon until the Lebanese army demonstrates it can independently prevent Hezbollah from rearming.

title: Sikh Union Ministers To Boycott Bhagwant Mann: BJP After 'Anti-Guru' Row
content: Punjab BJP chief Keval Singh Dhillon said the BJP's Sikh Union ministers will not hold meetings with Bhagwant Mann until he accepts the Akal Takht's directives and apologises

title: Census May Advance 2027 Assembly Polls In 5 States By Few Months: Sources
content: Since the elections in these five states are due around the same period that Census operations will be underway, the Commission may consider advancing polling dates by a few weeks or even a few months if required.

title: Decisions On Use of Force "Cannot Be Transferred to Machines": India At UN
content: Speaking at informal exchanges on artificial intelligence in the military domain held in Geneva on June 15, Indian Ambassador Charanjeet Singh said human judgement and oversight must be preserved "throughout the AI lifecycle", even as armed forces worldwide rapidly fold AI into their operations.

title: Even AI's Biggest Names Say Your Job Anxiety Is Justified
content: From Mustafa Suleyman to Demis Hassabis, some of AI's most influential figures say growing anxiety about jobs and the future of work is understandable.

title: Lion Mauls Man To Death In Gujarat, Severed Head, Body Parts Recovered
content: Assistant Conservator of Forests Viralsinh Chavda said a pride of four lions was moving in the area and efforts were underway to cage them all based on suspicion that one of them killed the man, who hailed from Uttarakhand.

title: LIVE Updates: Protests In Iran Over Deal, Netanyahu Says Israel Saved From Annihilation
content: US-Iran War LIVE Updates: Netanyahu pledged that Iran would never be allowed to acquire nuclear weapons, regardless of the terms of any agreement.

title: Iran Took Help From "Senior Psychologists" To Negotiate With Trump: Report
content: According to the Iranian official, who was not authorised to speak publicly, the psychiatric professionals began assisting Iranian negotiators following the initial round of bilateral talks in Islamabad in April.

title: Why 'military-grade' security? Annamalai, BJP leader lock horns over NEET-UG re-exam
content: The TOI News Desk comprises a dedicated and tireless team of journalists who operate around the clock to deliver the most current and comprehensive news and updates to the readers of The Times of India worldwide. With an unwavering commitment to excellence in journalism, our team is at the forefront of gathering, verifying, and presenting breaking news, in-depth analysis, and insightful reports on a wide range of topics. The TOI News Desk is your trusted source for staying informed and connected to the ever-evolving global landscape, ensuring that our readers are equipped with the latest developments that matter most." title: ‘Are you sure it exists?’ When flyers had to prove there’s actually a Noida airport
content: several passengers said airline and security personnel appeared unfamiliar with NCR’s newly opened airport . they needed to convince airport staff that their destination actually existed . some travellers found themselves explaining that Noida airport had begun commercial operations . others went a step further, pulling out news reports on their phones to prove it .
